03-14.text: Cubs sign Jason Heyward to an 8-year, $184 million deal, the biggest free agent signing in franchise history at the time.

03-25.text: The Chicago White Stockings (forerunners of the Cubs) become a charter member of the National League.

03-31.text: Kosuke Fukudome hits a game-tying three-run homer on Opening Day at Wrigley Field, sending the Friendly Confines into pandemonium.

04-02.text: Ryne Sandberg makes his first Opening Day start at second base, kicking off his MVP season.

04-04.text: Karl 'Tuffy' Rhodes smacks three home runs off Dwight Gooden on Opening Day at Wrigley, a Cubs record for the occasion.

04-07.text: Ernie Banks hits his 497th career home run on Opening Day at Wrigley Field as the Cubs beat the Phillies.

04-10.text: The Cubs play their first game at Weeghman Park, the stadium that would become Wrigley Field.

04-11.text: Greg Maddux fires a complete-game shutout on Opening Day at Wrigley Field, a sign of brilliance to come.

04-14.text: The Cubs retire Ernie Banks' No. 14, the first number retired in franchise history. Mr. Cub forever.

04-20.text: Ryne Sandberg goes 5-for-5 with two homers against the Cardinals. Just another day for Ryno.

04-23.text: Kris Bryant hits his first career MLB home run, launching a moonshot in San Diego that announced his arrival.

05-02.text: Hippo Vaughn and Fred Toney both throw no-hitters through nine innings in the same game. Vaughn loses the no-no in the 10th. The only double no-hitter in MLB history.

05-06.text: Kerry Wood strikes out 20 Astros in only his fifth career start, tying the MLB record. The most dominant single-game pitching performance in Cubs history.

05-12.text: Ernie Banks hits his 500th career home run off Pat Jarvis of the Braves at Wrigley Field. Mr. Cub reaches a milestone that cements his legend.

05-17.text: The Phillies beat the Cubs 23-22 in 10 innings at Wrigley Field, the highest-scoring game in modern MLB history. A beautiful disaster.

05-25.text: Babe Ruth hits the final three home runs of his career at Forbes Field, including the legendary 'called shot' predecessor. His 714th clears the roof.

06-03.text: Andre Dawson hits three home runs against the Phillies. The Hawk was an absolute menace all season en route to winning NL MVP.

06-15.text: The Cubs acquire Dennis Eckersley from the Red Sox in a trade. Eck would later become a Hall of Famer.

06-23.text: Ryne Sandberg goes off against the Cardinals in a nationally televised game, hitting two clutch home runs off Bruce Sutter. NBC's Game of the Week becomes 'The Sandberg Game' and makes Ryno a star.

06-29.text: Billy Williams extends his NL-record consecutive games played streak to 1,117. Iron Man of the Cubs.

07-02.text: Ferguson Jenkins fans 10 batters in a complete-game win, part of his run of six straight 20-win seasons for the Cubs.

07-08.text: The All-Star Game is played at Wrigley Field for the first time since 1962. The Friendly Confines hosts the Midsummer Classic.

07-17.text: Kris Bryant, Anthony Rizzo, Addison Russell, and Dexter Fowler all start in the All-Star Game. The Cubs take over the Midsummer Classic.

07-26.text: Carlos Zambrano throws a no-hitter against the Astros in Milwaukee (displaced by Hurricane Katrina). Big Z delivers a gem in a borrowed ballpark.

08-08.text: The first official night game at Wrigley Field. The Cubs beat the Mets 6-0 under the lights. Wrigley finally joins the modern era.
The Cubs celebrate the 30th anniversary of lights at Wrigley. What once seemed sacrilegious is now part of the charm.

08-09.text: The first attempted night game on 8/8 was rained out in the 4th inning vs. the Phillies. The Mets game on 8/9 became the first official night game completed at Wrigley.

08-14.text: Sammy Sosa is caught using a corked bat against the Devil Rays. A dark day for Slammin' Sammy's legacy.

08-18.text: Ron Santo makes his MLB debut at age 20 for the Cubs, beginning a legendary career on the North Side.

08-19.text: Jake Arrieta throws a no-hitter against the Dodgers in LA. The President was absolutely filthy all season long.

08-24.text: Kris Bryant hits three home runs in a single game against the Reds. KB puts on a power clinic at Great American Ball Park.

08-29.text: Bruce Sutter picks up his 30th save of the season. The split-finger assassin was virtually unhittable out of the Wrigley bullpen.

09-02.text: Milt Pappas comes within one strike of a perfect game against the Padres, losing it on a controversial walk. He settles for a no-hitter, but the perfecto that got away haunted him.

09-07.text: Anthony Rizzo hits his 30th home run of the season. Rizz was the heart and soul of the Cubs' championship run all year long.

09-08.text: Pete Rose gets hit No. 4,192 to break Ty Cobb's all-time record, with the Cubs on the wrong end of history at Riverfront Stadium.

09-09.text: Sandy Koufax throws a perfect game against the Cubs. Bob Hendley pitched a one-hitter in the loss. Possibly the best-pitched game in MLB history, and the Cubs still lost.

09-14.text: Sammy Sosa hits his 62nd home run, matching Mark McGwire's total in the legendary home run chase. Wrigley Field erupts.

09-16.text: The Cubs clinch the NL Central division title, their first division crown since 2008. The champagne flows in the Wrigley clubhouse.

09-22.text: Anthony Rizzo reaches 100 RBI for the third consecutive season. The most complete first baseman in the NL.

09-25.text: Sammy Sosa launches his 66th home run of the season. He finished the year with 66 dingers in one of the wildest HR races ever.

09-28.text: The Cubs clinch the NLDS against the Braves with a Game 5 win. Kerry Wood closes it out at Turner Field to send the Cubs to the NLCS.

10-01.text: Babe Ruth's 'Called Shot' at Wrigley Field in Game 3 of the World Series. Did he point to center field? The debate rages on, but the legend is forever.
A local tavern owner and his pet billy goat are turned away from Wrigley during the World Series. The 'Curse of the Billy Goat' is born.

10-02.text: The Cubs win Game 1 of the NLCS against the Padres 13-0. Rick Sutcliffe dominated and the North Side thought this was finally the year.

10-07.text: Steve Garvey's walk-off homer in Game 4 of the NLCS keeps the Padres alive. The Cubs' 2-0 series lead starts slipping away.
The Cubs beat the Braves to advance to the NLCS. The Friendly Confines is buzzing with October baseball.

10-08.text: The Cubs blank the Giants 1-0 in the NL Wild Card Game. Jake Arrieta shuts down San Francisco to kick off the postseason run of a lifetime.

10-13.text: Game 6 of the NLCS. Steve Bartman reaches for a foul ball, the Cubs collapse in the 8th inning, and the Marlins rally for an 8-3 win. The most painful night in Cubs history.

10-14.text: Game 7 of the NLCS. The Marlins finish off the Cubs 9-6 at Wrigley. So close, yet so far. The curse lives on.

10-15.text: The Cubs complete the NLDS sweep of the Giants with a 6-5 win. Javier Baez's solo homer in the 8th proves decisive. On to the NLCS.

10-21.text: The Cubs advance to the NLCS by sweeping the Cardinals. Daniel Murphy and the Mets await, but beating St. Louis in the playoffs is always sweet.

10-22.text: The Cubs win the NLCS pennant over the Dodgers. They're going to the World Series for the first time since 1945. Wrigley Field loses its collective mind.

10-25.text: World Series Game 1. The Cubs fall to the Indians 6-0 at Progressive Field. A rough start, but this team had comeback in its DNA.

10-28.text: World Series Game 3 at Wrigley Field. The first Fall Classic game at the Friendly Confines since 1945. The Cubs lose 1-0 but Wrigley is alive with World Series baseball again.

10-30.text: Addison Russell hits a grand slam in World Series Game 6 as the Cubs destroy the Indians 9-3 to force a Game 7. The North Side believes.

11-02.text: THE CUBS WIN THE WORLD SERIES. Game 7 in Cleveland, 8-7 in 10 innings. Ben Zobrist's go-ahead double, Miguel Montero's RBI single, and the rain delay. 108 years of heartbreak, over. The greatest night in Chicago Cubs history.

11-03.text: Chicago wakes up to a new reality: the Cubs are World Champions. The city celebrates with an estimated 5 million people at the victory parade along Lake Shore Drive and Grant Park.

11-17.text: Kris Bryant is named NL MVP after leading the Cubs to the World Series title. The kid from Las Vegas delivered on every ounce of hype.
